home.kpi_summary: SIIP tracks 6,798 ventures across 12 sectors. 1,032 achieved successful exits (15.2%), while 2,872 remain active (42.2%).
home.portfolio_health: Total capital deployed: $1137.6B across 8 regions. Median company age: 6 years.

pipeline.data_quality: Dataset: 6,798 ventures × 53 features. 395 ESG values imputed (median). Feature engineering produced 6 composite interaction terms.
pipeline.arima: ARIMA(2,1,2) models deal flow volume over time, forecasting pipeline capacity 12 months ahead. Seasonal decomposition reveals cyclical patterns in SWF investment activity.

regression.overview: Testing whether 17 structural venture features can linearly predict outcome categories (0-3 ordinal scale). R²=0.062 — statistically significant (F=26.27, p<1e-81) but explains only 6.2% of variance.
regression.ols_finding: Only funding_stage_num (p<0.001) and is_pre_revenue (p=0.027) reach statistical significance. The model captures real but limited linear signal — structural venture attributes have weak linear relationships with outcomes.
regression.lasso_finding: Lasso zeroed 13/17 features — only funding maturity (β=0.212), political stability interaction (β=0.029), patents (β=0.003), and company age (β<0.001) retain any linear predictive power.
regression.key_finding: R²≈6% IS the finding, not a failure. Linear models are structurally limited for categorical outcomes. This motivates the shift to non-linear classification models that can capture complex feature interactions.
regression.bridge: → Prediction Engine uses non-linear classifiers (Decision Trees, Random Forest, XGBoost) to improve on linear limitations.

classification.imbalance: Success rate = 15.2% (6:1 class imbalance). Without correction, a naive classifier achieves 84.8% accuracy by always predicting failure. All models use balanced class weights or scale_pos_weight.
classification.best_model: Best classifier: Logistic Regression (AUC-ROC = 0.630). Non-linear models significantly outperform linear regression (R²=6%) by capturing complex feature interactions.
classification.shap: SHAP analysis on Gradient Boosting reveals which features drive individual predictions — enabling analysts to understand WHY a venture is flagged as promising or risky.
classification.threshold: At optimal threshold 0.55: F1=0.311. False negatives (missing winners) are costlier than false positives (extra due diligence), so lower thresholds may be strategically preferred.

clustering.overview: Segmentation identifies natural venture archetypes. K-Means optimal k=2 (silhouette=0.151). Each archetype has distinct risk-return profiles requiring tailored strategies.
clustering.pca: PCA: 2 components explain 34.7% of total variance. PC1 captures stability_x_stage, PC2 captures funding_stage_num.
clustering.anomaly: Isolation Forest flagged 340 ventures (5.0%) as anomalous. Anomaly success rate: 27.6% vs normal 14.5%.

arm.overview: Market-basket analysis adapted for venture screening. 14 continuous features discretized into tercile bins. 71052 total association rules discovered.
arm.success_patterns: 428 rules predict successful exits. Strongest patterns reveal which feature combinations are most predictive of success.
arm.anti_patterns: 540 rules predict write-offs — these serve as automated red-flag screening filters.

deal_evaluator.overview: Synthesizes all models into a single venture assessment: classification probability, cluster membership, anomaly status, and matching ARM patterns.
deal_evaluator.methodology: Score combines Logistic Regression probability (0.630 AUC), cluster persona context, and 2244 association rules.
